findings.problem: The confrontation runs for more than ten consecutive paragraphs with almost no Kem interiority. Once the accusation crests, the chapter records dialogue, silence, and blocking from outside, but it rarely lets the reader feel what each admission is doing inside Kem. In a Kem POV chapter that is supposed to trap the reader in betrayed perspective, this much screenplay-mode externalization thins the emotional pressure right where the chapter should be most claustrophobic.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the exchange centered on snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:101, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:113, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:123 so Vel's pauses and concessions are broken by brief Kem-local processing: bodily recoil, level-hum awareness, memory collision, self-disgust, or tactical recalculation. Keep the scene in confrontation, but thread interior beats between the admissions instead of letting the whole stretch play as exterior dialogue until snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:133.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised passage covering the current stretch from snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:101 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:131, there are at least three distinct Kem-interior beats tied to Vel's answers, and there is no run of 10 or more consecutive paragraphs that contains only external action/dialogue with no thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment from Kem.

findings.problem: Kem's central accusation is sharper than human speech pressure supports. Several turns arrive as balanced mini-speeches and quotable thematic formulations rather than anger working its way out in real time: 'Component,' 'range. Budget. Whether I'd last,' and 'Nicer reason, same thing' all sound authored to land cleanly. The narrator's own 'The word landed clean and ugly' compounds the sense that the scene is admiring how the lines hit instead of letting messy speech generate the force.
findings.rewrite_direction: Roughen the confrontation around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:81, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:87, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:99, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:107 so Kem still makes the same moral accusation, but through pressure-marked speech true to his style profile: questions, self-corrections, repeated words, concrete callbacks to the road and the training, and at least one turn that arrives unfinished once the meaning lands. Cut or de-polish narrator-admiring phrasing at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:61 if the scene no longer needs it.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised confrontation spanning the current lines snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:81 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:107, at least two of Kem's longer turns show pressure on the syntax through interruption, restart, self-correction, or incompletely finished grammar, and the accusation is carried by concrete scene particulars rather than a final abstract equivalence sentence like the current line at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:107.

findings.problem: The chapter's strongest concrete image already makes its moral point when Kem sees the labor notice and connects Vel's arithmetic to Hoarded yield logic. After that, the scene keeps paraphrasing the same idea in cleaner thematic language instead of moving the confrontation into a fresh cost or consequence. That underlining blunts the force of the notice, violates the style bible's ban on thematic self-translation, and makes the back half of the exchange feel more explained than discovered.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten and restage the passage beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:97. Let the notice plus one accusation do the conceptual work, then use the next beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:107 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:123 to force something new: a more specific admission from Vel, a concrete relational break, or a choice that changes what the trio does next. Remove any sentence in this stretch whose only job is to restate the Hoarded-system parallel more abstractly than the image at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:97 already has.
findings.acceptance_test: After the revised equivalent of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:97, each subsequent beat in the current span through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:131 must add a new fact, concession, or consequence on the page. If two successive beats can both be summarized as 'Kem says Vel used him like the Hoarded use people' without any new operational or relational change, the revision fails.

summary: Chapter 13 lands the required reveal, keeps continuity clean, and does not sanitize Vel's manipulation, but it still reads below pass level for this review stage. The reveal scene spends too long outside Kem's interior, the confrontation gives him several overly composed thesis-lines, and the prose keeps re-stating the Margins parallel after the labor notice has already made it legible.
